meta.wire_notation: colour1/colour2 = main colour / stripe colour. Dimension in mm² before colour.

unit_notes.track: Current track number (vertical column in diagram)

diagram_reading_guide.title: Sample current flow diagram with explanatory notes
notes: All switches and contacts are illustrated in the mechanical OFF position.
The grey area represents the relay plate with fuse holder.
Wire cross section in mm² is shown as a number on the wire (e.g. 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.5, 4.0, 6.0, 16.0, 25.0, 35.0).
Wiring colours are shown in the actual colours found on the vehicle.
Numbers/number combinations indicate individual contacts in a multi-point connector, e.g. T10/4 = ten-point connector, contact 4.
Numbers in yellow squares indicate that a wire is discontinued in the diagram and refer to the current track where it is continued.
Terminals with numbers are the numbers on the actual parts (e.g. 15, 30, 31, 50, 56, 58).
Symbols (e.g. W = interior light) – using the legend you can find which part is referred to.
Numbers in circles indicate the locations of earthing points (see legend).
Current track numbers at the bottom help find parts in the diagram (see legend).
Pictorial illustrations of parts whose symbols are given in the current track above.
Internal connections (thin lines) are NOT found in the form of wires. They are however current-carrying connections, making it possible to check the flow of current within a component or unit.

ignition_starter_switch.title: Ignition/starter switch viewed from below

terminals.function: Accessory circuit (switched, also in accessory position)
To alternator warning light / charge indicator

ignition_starter_switch.notes: The switch controls which power rails are energised. Terminal 30 is always live from battery. Terminal 15 provides switched ignition power to fuses/relays. Terminal X provides accessory power.

earth_points.title: Earthing points – location and function
earth_points.description: Satisfactory operation of the starter motor, ignition system and electrical accessories depends upon the provision of clean, secure earth bonds.

symptoms.symptom: Starter motor fails to turn engine
reasons: Battery terminal leads loose or earth lead not securely attached to body
Loose or broken connections in starter motor circuit
Starter motor switch or solenoid faulty
Starter brushes badly worn, sticking, or brush wires loose

symptoms.symptom: Starter motor turns engine very slowly
reasons: Starter brushes badly worn, sticking, or brush wires loose
Loose wires in starter motor circuit

symptoms.symptom: Starter motor operates without turning engine
reasons: Pinion or flywheel gear teeth broken or worn

symptoms.symptom: Starter motor noisy or has an excessively rough engagement
reasons: Pinion or flywheel gear teeth broken or worn

symptoms.symptom: Battery will not hold charge for more than a few days
reasons: Electrolyte level too low or electrolyte too weak due to leakage
Plate separators no longer fully effective
Battery terminal connections loose or corroded
Short in lighting circuit causing continual battery drain

symptoms.symptom: Ignition light fails to go out; battery runs flat in a few days
reasons: Alternator belt loose and slipping or broken
Cable between tank sender unit and gauge earthed or loose

symptoms.symptom: Fuel gauge registers full all the time
reasons: Cable between tank unit and gauge broken or disconnected
Horn push either earthed or stuck down
Horn cable to horn push earthed
Cable or cable connection loose, broken or disconnected

symptoms.symptom: Horn emits intermittent or unsatisfactory noise

reasons: If engine not running, battery discharged
Light bulb filament burnt out or bulbs broken
Wire connections loose, disconnected or broken
Light switch shorting or otherwise faulty

symptoms.symptom: Lights come on but fade out
reasons: If engine not running, battery discharged
Incorrect bulb with too low wattage fitted
Existing bulbs old and badly discoloured
Electrical wiring too thin not allowing full current to pass

symptoms.symptom: Lights work erratically – flashing on and off, especially over bumps
reasons: Battery terminals or earth connection loose
Wire connections loose, disconnected or broken

symptoms.symptom: Wiper motor works very slowly and takes excessive current
reasons: Drive to wheelboxes bent or unlubricated

symptoms.symptom: Wiper motor works slowly and takes little current
Wiper motor works but wiper blades remain static
reasons: Wheelbox gear and spindle damaged or worn
Wiper motor gearbox parts badly worn

washer_system.title: Electric washer system

headlamp_jet_adjustment.setting: 24.0 mm (0.94 in) from centre of jet to centre of lamp lens

dimensions.c: Manual 50.0 mm (1.96 in), electric 200.0 mm (7.9 in)
dimensions.d: Manual 50.0 mm (1.96 in), electric 200.0 mm (7.9 in)

maintenance_notes: All later models have electrically-operated windscreen washer.
Some versions also have headlamp washers.
Early models have a foot-operated pump.
Maintain fluid level and use washer cleaning liquid or methylated spirit in freezing temperatures.
Never add cooling system antifreeze to washer fluid.
Check security of washer tubes and electrical leads.

notes: Certain models are fitted with a driving cab heated rear window.
A relay for this electrical accessory is located on the fuse block.
Avoid scratching the heater element and do not stick labels over it.
Clean the glass with water only, and rub in the direction of the element heater strips.
It may be possible to repair a break in the heater element by using a proprietary conductive paint. Failing this, renewal of the window will be necessary.

fig_10_35.title: Supplementary wiring diagram for headlight washers
fig_10_35.applies: Early models (pre-1981 supplement) with headlight washers

component_key.description: Windscreen wiper switch for intermittent operation
To headlight dip and flasher relay, terminal 56
Connector, two-point, near headlight washer pump

wires.description: Relay power from terminal 56 via J
Relay output to headlight washer pump
Light switch enables headlight washer relay

fig_10_35.functional_description: Early headlight washer system. J39 relay is energised when: (1) lights are on (E1 terminal 56 powers relay coil), AND (2) washer is activated via E22 terminal 53c. J39 then powers V11 headlight washer pump via heavy 4.0mm² ro (red) wire. V11 pump has 4.0mm² br (brown) earth wire due to high current draw. V5 windscreen washer pump uses lighter 0.5mm² earth. Note that J31 (wash/wipe intermittent relay) position is reserved but may not be fitted on all models.
fig_10_35.differences_from_fig_13_80: Fig 10.35 is the original pre-supplement version. Fig 13.80 (page 289) is the updated version in Chapter 13 Supplement with revised relay designations and connector references. The basic function is identical. Key difference: Fig 10.35 shows J39 with terminals S, 31, P and 4.0mm² pump wiring clearly visible. Fig 13.80 uses the later connector notation (T1a, T1b, T2 etc.).

chapter_13_supplement_generic.title: Supplementary wiring diagram for headlight washers

ground_points.location: Earth point, behind dash, near fuse box

wires.description: Power supply from X rail to fuse

chapter_13_supplement_generic.functional_description: When the windscreen washer is activated via E22, J31 relay controls the wash-wipe sequence. J39 is energised simultaneously to power V11 headlight washer pump via M20. The headlight washers only operate when the headlights are on (via E1 terminal 56 enabling J39 coil circuit).

chapter_13_supplement_generic.title: Supplementary wiring diagram for front and rear foglights

ground_points.location: Earth point, on cross panel/side member
Earth point, behind dash on left
Earth point, behind dash on right

wires.description: To single connector behind cross panel

chapter_13_supplement_generic.functional_description: Foglights can only be operated when parking/dipped beam is on (E1 terminal 58 enables E23). J5 relay switches the main foglight current. E23 switch has two stages: first click = front fogs via 83a, second click = rear fog via 83b. K13 warning lamp illuminates when rear fog is active. S28 (8A) protects the fog circuit.

chapter_13_supplement_generic.title: Supplementary wiring diagram for front and rear foglights

components.description: Foglight relay on aux. relay adaptor
Front and rear foglight switch bulb
Fuse, terminal 58b, on aux. relay adaptor

connectors.description: Connector, single, behind air vent trim
Connector, single, behind air vent trim

ground_points.location: Earth point, on dash panel insert
Earth point, behind dash panel, right
Earth point, next to relay plate

chapter_13_supplement_generic.functional_description: Updated foglight circuit for 1986+ models. J5 relay now on auxiliary relay adaptor. S50 provides fused 58b supply for instrument illumination (R radio, L10 dash lights, L28 cigarette lighter light, L40 foglight switch light). Fog switch E23 requires E1 terminal 58 to be active. K17 warning lamp indicates fog operation.

chapter_13_supplement_generic.title: Supplementary wiring diagram for dual circuit brakes and handbrake warning lamp
chapter_13_supplement_generic.title_sv: Tilläggskopplingsschema för dubbelkrets broms och handbromsvarningslampa

components.description: Oil pressure switch (to oil pressure switch)
Dual circuit and handbrake warning lamp

wires.description: Brake switch to left brake light
Brake switch to right brake light

chapter_13_supplement_generic.functional_description: S6 fuse provides permanent 30 supply to brake light switch F. When brake pedal is pressed, F sends current to M9 and M10 brake lights. K7 warning lamp is triggered by: F9 handbrake switch (handbrake on), or hydraulic brake pressure differential switch (brake circuit failure). F1 oil pressure switch shares earth path.

chapter_13_supplement_generic.title: Supplementary wiring diagram for fusing of lights in dash with rev counter
chapter_13_supplement_generic.title_sv: Tilläggskopplingsschema för säkring av instrumentbelysning med varvräknare
chapter_13_supplement_generic.functional_description: S6 provides main fused supply. E20 controls instrument panel brightness via S50 fuse on relay adaptor. S50 feeds terminal 58b to all panel illumination: L10 dash inserts, L8 clock, L16 fresh air, L28 cigarette lighter, L39 heated rear window switch, and R radio backlight. G5 rev counter receives power via T1a connector. All illumination uses 0.5mm² gr/bl (grey/blue) wires.

chapter_13_supplement_generic.title: Supplementary wiring diagram for fusing of lights in dash with clock and tachograph
chapter_13_supplement_generic.title_sv: Tilläggskopplingsschema för säkring av instrumentbelysning med klocka och färdskrivare
chapter_13_supplement_generic.functional_description: Identical to Fig 13.84 but with G24 tachograph replacing G5 rev counter, and Y clock added. S6 fuse provides main supply. E20 dims panel lights via S50. All illumination on 0.5mm² gr/bl wires. G24 tachograph connects via T2b 2-pin connector behind dash.

chapter_13_supplement_generic.title: Supplementary wiring diagram for trailer towing

connectors.description: Pin 4 on 14-pin connector on dash panel insert

wires.description: Trailer lamp control from dash connector

chapter_13_supplement_generic.functional_description: Trailer towing harness taps into existing lighting circuits. S14 (8A) and S13 (8A) provide fused supply for trailer lights. Turn signals, tail lights, and brake lights are connected in parallel with vehicle lights. K18 on dash indicates trailer connection status. All trailer wires are typically 1.0mm². Earth via pins 31 to rear crossmember ground points 18/19.
